settings.title: Cupping Evenings Are Back, First Thursday of the Month
settings.excerpt: Join us after hours to taste through new arrivals the way we do behind the bar.
settings.body:
Our monthly cupping evenings return this month. It is a relaxed, guided tasting of the coffees we are considering for the bar, run the same way we evaluate beans behind the scenes.

You will smell and slurp your way through four or five coffees, compare notes with the room, and leave with a sharper palate. No experience needed, and curiosity is the only requirement.

What a cupping evening looks like

We line up small bowls of each coffee, ground fresh and steeped in hot water with nothing added. You break the crust with a spoon, lean in for the aroma, then slurp a spoonful so it sprays across your tongue. It feels a little odd the first time and entirely normal by the third bowl. There are no scores to memorise and no right answers, just what you notice and how it changes as the coffee cools.

Tasting side by side is the part most people remember. A coffee that seems ordinary on its own can read as bright and fruity next to a heavier neighbour, and the contrast is what trains your palate fastest.

A few practical notes

  • Sessions run on the first Thursday of the month, after we close the bar.
  • Come with a clean palate, so go easy on anything strongly flavoured beforehand.
  • Bring a notebook if you like, though we hand out scorecards too.

Sessions are free but space is limited. Sign up at the counter on your next visit, and let us know if it is your first time so we can pair you with someone who has done it before.
